segment.source: A compact KPI card pairing a single metric with a tiny inline trend line and an optional change badge, backed by a screen-reader table of every period and value. Use it in dashboards or stat sections to show a key figure and its recent direction without a full chart. Key fields are the series label, unit, the Period | Value data points, the chart-data JSON, the trend badge text, and a colour variant.
What the sparkline measures, e.g. "Weekly active users". Used as the data-table column header and announced to screen readers.
Short unit shown after every value, e.g. "users", "% MoM", or "k USD". Leave empty for plain numbers.
One point per line as "Period | Value", e.g. Jul | 42100. Keep them in time order. Drives the screen-reader data table; match the same labels and values in the chart data JSON below.
JSON array the sparkline is drawn from, in time order: [{"label":"Jul","value":42100}]. Match the labels and values entered under Data points.
Optional pill shown beside the title, e.g. "+12.4% vs last quarter". An up, down or flat arrow is added automatically from the net change.
Chart palette colour used for the sparkline and its fill.
{0}: {1} sparkline across {2, plural, one {# point} other {# points}} from {3} to {4}{5}. The full series follows in the data table.
